name: CMSE 404 Exam 3 — Non-Transformer Topics
goal: Master Decision Trees, Naive Bayes, Clustering, Pretrained LMs for CMSE 404 Exam 3 (2026-04-09)

history.notes: Diagnostic — doesn't know splitting metrics (entropy, info gain, Gini). Understands that a good split separates classes but can't articulate how the algorithm selects features.
After teaching: correctly computes Gini impurity. Forgot to weight children by sample proportion — computed individual child Gini correctly but didn't combine with (n_child/n_parent) weighting.

Diagnostic — knows Bayes theorem formula. Confused naive assumption with temporal independence instead of feature conditional independence. Doesn't know how NB decomposes joint probability for classification.

After teaching: correctly computes proportional class scores. Forgot to normalize to actual probabilities. Understands conditional independence assumption and why NB works despite violation — needs to remember to normalize on exam.

After teaching: correct assign-then-update procedure but made errors in distance calculation on toy example. Doesn't know K-means limitations (bad init, spherical assumption). Confused K-means with KNN initially.
Correctly executed 2D toy dataset assign+update. Only recalls initialization sensitivity as limitation — must memorize: choose K in advance, spherical cluster assumption.

Good grasp of BERT vs GPT architectural difference and attention patterns. Slightly vague on why GPT suits generation (attention pattern matches autoregressive generation). Didn't articulate that pretrain-then-fine-tune solves the small labeled data problem.
